title: One Is Silicon And The Other Gold

jokes.text: It's great to see so many happy smiles, most of them on Mouth Mutant. Hey-o!
jokes.setup: Generic wedding speech opener about happy faces
jokes.punchline: Subverted by the literal interpretation - one mutant has multiple mouths
jokes.explanation: Solid worldbuilding humor that plays on the show's mutant society. Clean setup/punchline with good economy.

jokes.text: even that year my side mutated into some kind of venomous radish
jokes.setup: Romantic speech about wife staying by his side through everything
jokes.punchline: Reveals his 'side' literally mutated into something bizarre
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that plays perfectly into the show's mutant concept while maintaining the romantic sentiment.

jokes.text: And those things never mutated.
jokes.setup: Praising Morris's honesty and humility
jokes.punchline: Contrasts his unchanging virtues with his literally mutating body
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback that deepens the previous joke while showing Munda's wit and the couple's dynamic.

jokes.text: Now, if you'll direct your attention to this moldy shower curtain
jokes.setup: Formal presentation setup for anniversary video
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're using a gross shower curtain as a screen
jokes.explanation: Solid poverty humor that establishes the sewer setting while contrasting formal presentation with gross reality.

jokes.text: Well, not that much, actually, since mutants weren't allowed to leave the sewers until last year. Like anyone wants to leave the sewer. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Leela narrating about her parents going through 'so much'
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were literally trapped underground, Morris defends it unconvincingly
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Morris's defensive nature and the show's social commentary on mutant segregation.

jokes.text: I still have no idea.
jokes.setup: Narration explains Morris didn't know what exolinguistics meant
jokes.punchline: Morris confirms he still doesn't after 40 years
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Morris's willful ignorance with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Oh, sugar lump, I hear you when you actually say something important.
jokes.setup: Munda frustrated that Morris never listens about her field
jokes.punchline: Morris dismisses her life's passion as unimportant while using pet name
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Morris's obliviousness wrapped in condescending affection.

jokes.text: like the fairy tale of face mutant and butt mutant
jokes.setup: Romantic narration about opposites attracting
jokes.punchline: References a fairy tale about anatomically-specific mutants
jokes.explanation: Creative worldbuilding humor that plays on classic fairy tale tropes with mutant twist.

jokes.text: Pot-roast-burning.
jokes.setup: Munda mentions her 'lucrative pot-roast-making field'
jokes.punchline: Morris casually corrects it to burning, revealing her failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with maximum economy. Morris's casual correction is brutally honest.

jokes.text: I had dreams, too! I was going to surf the world's sewers, but I happily gave it up for you. Happily!
jokes.setup: Morris defending his sacrifices for love
jokes.punchline: The repeated 'happily' reveals his obvious resentment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing repressed resentment. The repetition sells the lie perfectly.

jokes.text: Divorce is final.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to calm them down during their quarrel
jokes.punchline: Morris immediately announces divorce with no buildup
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation with perfect economy. Morris's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know, the rents are a lot more reasonable in Peru.
jokes.setup: Morris explaining his temporary living arrangement
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Peru as a housing option in their sewer society
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor suggesting global sewer connectivity. Casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Dance, slave, dance.
jokes.setup: Scene at what appears to be an upscale establishment
jokes.punchline: Robot commanding someone to dance as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag that establishes the scene but lacks originality or strong character integration.

jokes.text: Imagine. Me, a girl from the sewer meeting a man made entirely of detergent.
jokes.setup: Munda expressing wonder at meeting someone
jokes.punchline: Romantic description of a literally soap-based person
jokes.explanation: Creative character interaction with good worldbuilding. The romance-novel tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, I have an idea. Let's look in a different direction at something else.
jokes.setup: Munda pointing out Zapp Brannigan
jokes.punchline: Leela's obvious attempt to avoid her past with Zapp
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor showing Leela's desperation to avoid Zapp. Natural delivery.

jokes.text: That's a napkin dispenser, sir. This is the universal translator.
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to conduct important diplomatic business
jokes.punchline: Reveals Zapp was talking to restaurant equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp incompetence with Kif's long-suffering correction. Perfect character dynamics.

jokes.text: Whatever.
jokes.setup: Kif correcting him about the napkin dispenser vs translator
jokes.punchline: Zapp's dismissive response to being wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp characterization - his arrogant dismissal of being corrected. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Table Seven?
jokes.setup: Zapp grandly announcing 'Treaty of Table Seven'
jokes.punchline: Kif's confused reaction to the arbitrary name
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that highlights how Zapp makes up important-sounding names for mundane things.

jokes.text: Last time you tried that, the Mexican restaurant declared war on us.
jokes.setup: Zapp wanting to speak in native tongue despite past failures
jokes.punchline: Reveals previous diplomatic incident at casual dining
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the absurd consequences of Zapp's incompetence. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'd like to spank your sister with a slice of bologna.
jokes.setup: Zapp attempting to congratulate aliens in their language
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says something bizarre and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific mistranslation that's both absurd and character-perfect. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It's the Battle of Paco's Tacos all over again.
jokes.setup: Diplomatic disaster unfolding
jokes.punchline: References another restaurant-based military conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the restaurant war concept. Great worldbuilding and very quotable.

jokes.text: Just that Captain Brannigan confused the word for 'congratulations' with the word for 'spank your sister with bologna.'
jokes.setup: Explaining what Zapp said to resolve the conflict
jokes.punchline: Munda matter-of-factly explains the bizarre mistranslation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Munda's competence while reinforcing the absurdity.

jokes.text: So, yesterday, huh? That business happened, right? Somebody?
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to justify corporate expense
jokes.punchline: Desperately vague attempt to create fake business discussion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment showing his transparent desperation and poor social skills.

jokes.text: I don't know where most of her is, but her tongue's in Zapp's mouth.
jokes.setup: Leela asking where her mom went
jokes.punchline: Fry describes witnessing them making out
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with efficient setup and payoff. Fry's matter-of-fact delivery helps.

jokes.text: Show me where he touched you on this dried-apple doll.
jokes.setup: Leela checking if Zapp took advantage of her mom
jokes.punchline: Uses therapy/police procedure props inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that plays on serious situations absurdly. Good concept but slightly awkward execution.

jokes.text: Did you know he wears a velour man-skirt?
jokes.setup: Munda praising Zapp as handsome and gentlemanly
jokes.punchline: Reveals his questionable fashion choice as a positive
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Munda's infatuation while mocking Zapp's style.

jokes.text: I know. He showed me the commemorative painting.
jokes.setup: Leela revealing she slept with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Munda already knows because Zapp showed evidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the situation more awkward. Zapp having a painting is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Although he did say I'm a better kisser.
jokes.setup: Munda reassuring Leela this isn't a competition
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes it competitive with comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction showing Munda's unconscious competitiveness. Great structure.

jokes.text: You know, without your mother, the sewer's a dank and dismal place.
jokes.setup: Morris expressing how much he misses Munda
jokes.punchline: Describes the sewer as if it was previously pleasant
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor that shows Morris's selective memory about their living conditions.

jokes.text: I ought to punch him right in his big mouth. And then in all his smaller mouths!
jokes.setup: Morris wanting to punch his wife's new man
jokes.punchline: Escalates to punching multiple mouths, referencing Mouth Mutant
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with nice callback to earlier character. Shows Morris's anger building logically.

jokes.text: and they can make sure you don't...
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting Fry and Bender accompany Morris on his trip
jokes.punchline: Trails off, implying Morris needs supervision for safety
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from implication but not particularly strong. Shows concern without being explicit.

jokes.text: What? It's 2:00 in the afternoon. Several days later.
jokes.setup: Leela asking where he's been
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that days have passed unnoticed
jokes.explanation: Good time-passage gag that shows Morris has been completely absorbed in surfing.

jokes.text: You're too old to be happy.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to convince her mom to break up with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Bluntly cruel argument about age and happiness
jokes.explanation: Shockingly blunt and cruel line that shows Leela's desperation. Very quotable dark humor.

jokes.text: Sex with Zapp! Sex with Zapp! Sex with Zapp!
jokes.setup: Munda defending her relationship with increasingly personal details
jokes.punchline: Repeats the most uncomfortable detail for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor through repetition. Gets the discomfort across but lacks craft.

jokes.text: This one is for the E. coli. Ow! And this one's for the leprechaunorrhea. Hiddly-dee!
jokes.setup: Fry getting medical shots for sewer diseases
jokes.punchline: Combines STD with leprechaun, adds appropriate exclamation
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau with good commitment to the bit. The leprechaun sound effect sells it.

jokes.text: the wind ripening in your face
jokes.setup: Morris describing the zen of sewer surfing
jokes.punchline: Says 'ripening' instead of 'whipping', referencing sewer smells
jokes.explanation: Clever malapropism that works both as mistake and accurate sewer description. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I don't want to put a rat in your face-cage, or whatever you kids say nowadays
jokes.setup: Morris trying to use young people's slang
jokes.punchline: Completely garbles 'rain on your parade' with disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent malapropism that's both disturbing and perfectly wrong. Shows Morris trying to relate to youth.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't my second favorite Turanga girl.
jokes.setup: Zapp greeting Leela
jokes.punchline: Ranks her below her mother while trying to be charming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp line that's insulting while trying to be smooth. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Shall we say about 7:58? Precisely?
jokes.setup: Zapp agreeing to dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and awkward timing
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Zapp's weird preciseness about unimportant things while being incompetent about important ones.

jokes.text: Leela, does your mother know you're using her pole?
jokes.setup: Leela trying to seduce Zapp away from her mother
jokes.punchline: Zapp makes inappropriate comment about the situation
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo that's more uncomfortable than funny. Fits character but lacks craft.

jokes.text: She is one juicy calamari.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining his love for Munda
jokes.punchline: Describes her in gross food terms relating to her tentacle
jokes.explanation: Gross but character-appropriate description. Zapp's food obsessions make it work.

jokes.text: Leela, here's $40.
jokes.setup: Zapp and Munda heading to motel room
jokes.punchline: Gives Leela money implying she's a prostitute or needs to leave
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that's more cringe than funny. Works for character but limited craft.

jokes.text: Just call me, 'Daddy.'
jokes.setup: Leela calling Zapp names
jokes.punchline: Zapp wants to be called daddy as her step-father
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable moment that escalates the family dynamic awkwardness.

jokes.text: Probably, it'll be a filthy nurse.
jokes.setup: Leela threatening to put mom in nursing home so fast she won't know what hit her
jokes.punchline: Specifies it'll likely be an abusive caregiver
jokes.explanation: Dark follow-up that adds specific threat detail. Shows Leela's anger escalating.

jokes.text: I was riding the El Torito dinner backwash, and I thought I'd pop in.
jokes.setup: Morris arriving at apartment casually
jokes.punchline: Describes surfing restaurant waste water as normal activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with specific restaurant chain reference. Perfect Morris characterization.

jokes.text: So? He seems like a nice kahuna.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining Mom's going to marry Zapp
jokes.punchline: Morris uses surfer slang inappropriately for 'guy'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Morris's new surfer persona affecting his language.

jokes.text: So? He seems like a nice kahuna.
jokes.setup: Learning about Zapp marrying Munda
jokes.punchline: Using surfer term inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Morris misusing surfer terminology. Shows his transformation is surface-level.

jokes.text: You got to take the good with the gnarly.
jokes.setup: Morris giving life philosophy advice
jokes.punchline: Mixes 'take the good with the bad' with surfer slang
jokes.explanation: Clever mixing of cliche wisdom with surf terminology. Shows Morris's personality shift well.

jokes.text: time for me to shoot the IHOP dessert curl.
jokes.setup: Morris about to leave
jokes.punchline: References surfing pancake house waste as if it's a famous surf spot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor combining surf culture with chain restaurant waste. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Maybe Zapp isn't the only one who's marriage material.
jokes.setup: Fry giving relationship advice to Leela
jokes.punchline: Thinks this is his moment to propose romantic potential
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry misreading of social situations. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Really? I'm not showing too much sucker for a lady my age?
jokes.setup: Leela complimenting her wedding appearance
jokes.punchline: Munda worried about showing too much tentacle inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that plays on both age insecurity and alien anatomy humorously.

jokes.text: So I will now join myself in holy macaroni.
jokes.setup: Zapp conducting his own wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Says 'macaroni' instead of 'matrimony'
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp malapropism that fits his food obsession. Simple but effective character humor.

jokes.text: I come from the sewer, but that is low.
jokes.setup: Munda learning about Zapp's deceptive war plan
jokes.punchline: Someone from literal sewers calling something morally low
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic humor with perfect economy. The literal/metaphorical 'low' contrast is brilliant and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Careful! That's glass!
jokes.setup: Munda angrily throwing away engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Zapp more concerned about fake ring than relationship ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment showing his misplaced priorities and cheapness even in dramatic moments.

jokes.text: Then, once again, my lies have been proven true.
jokes.setup: Aliens declaring war after Zapp's deception is revealed
jokes.punchline: Zapp claims vindication through circular reasoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Zapp logic that's completely wrong but internally consistent. Perfect character moment and highly quotable.

jokes.text: you can't return ice cream cakes!
jokes.setup: Zapp ordering Kif to return fire and cake
jokes.punchline: Kif points out the logistical impossibility of cake returns
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Kif's literal interpretation during crisis. Shows their dynamic well.

jokes.text: you can't return ice cream cakes!
jokes.setup: Zapp ordering Kif to return fire and cake
jokes.punchline: Kif's practical objection about cake logistics during battle
jokes.explanation: Great Kif moment - focusing on practical impossibilities while under attack. Perfect deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: But then I conquered my inner peace.
jokes.setup: Morris explaining he was zen about the situation
jokes.punchline: Treating inner peace as an enemy to defeat
jokes.explanation: Excellent paradoxical humor that subverts spiritual concepts. Very quotable and perfectly captures Morris's character arc.

jokes.text: But then I conquered my inner peace.
jokes.setup: Morris explaining he was zen about the situation at first
jokes.punchline: Describes achieving inner peace as something to overcome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction of zen philosophy with military language. Perfect character development moment and very quotable.

jokes.text: If heaven's above us, then the whole universe is just God's glorious sewer.
jokes.setup: Morris overcoming his fear of space
jokes.punchline: Reframes the universe in sewer terms to make it less scary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical reframing that's both touching and hilarious. Perfect character moment that transforms fear into courage through familiar metaphor. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: Agnew, give it your stamp of approval.
jokes.setup: Peace treaty being signed
jokes.punchline: Nixon's vice president literally stamps the document
jokes.explanation: Solid historical reference humor with literal interpretation of 'stamp of approval.' Good but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Uh... Half a cake.
jokes.setup: Morris mentioning they still have wedding cake
jokes.punchline: Leela corrects that it was damaged in battle
jokes.explanation: Minor callback that acknowledges battle damage. Functional but not particularly funny.
